translation: And Ozias begot Joatham; and Joatham begot Achaz; and Achaz begot Hezekiah.

alternate_translations.translation: Uzziah fathered Jotham, and Jotham fathered Ahaz, and Ahaz fathered Hezekiah.
alternate_translations.notes: “Begot” and “fathered” both convey parental descent in a genealogical list; this version uses more idiomatic English while keeping the meaning.

alternate_translations.translation: Ozias became the father of Joatham, Joatham became the father of Achaz, and Achaz became the father of Hezekiah.
alternate_translations.notes: This is a more explicit rephrasing of the same genealogical “A begot B” structure, common in smooth English renditions of biblical lists.

notes: This is a genealogical succession formula from the biblical tradition. The Greek forms of the names (Ὀζίας, Ἰωαθάμ, Ἄχαζ, Ἑζεκίαν) correspond to the more familiar English names (Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah). Spelling varies by translation due to transliteration conventions from Greek/Latin into English.
